site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Round 7 setup board for tipping, charity betting, and weekly NRL intel
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 7 preview notes, charity betting discipline, and weekly NRL intel.

ticker: official round 7 team lists are now out and the board can sharpen up
bulldogs over eels is the cleanest early lean after the list drop
rabbitohs lose jye gray while dragons lose moses suli from the starting side
tigers get jarome luai back while broncos name reynolds but still lose carrigan to suspension
cowboys are without murray taulagi and reed mahoney against manly
charity record stays at 2 and 0 so there is still no need to force an early bet

featurePanels.title: Lock Pick
items: No Round 7 charity bet locked yet
Bulldogs over Eels is the strongest early lean
Eels lost more bodies again while Leo Thompson returns for Canterbury
Still wait for market shape before calling it the charity play

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: Panthers, Storm and Eels all burned the board
Favourite logic got exposed hard in a volatile round
Parra, Melbourne and the Dragons now need trust rebuilt, not assumed
Injury news still matters more than reputation this week
Keep treating the board as a briefing tool, not blind staking

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Decide whether Tigers are now a trust side or a trap side
See if the Warriors can back up their statement win with Capewell back
Test whether the Bulldogs are now a full-confidence team against Parra
Watch if the Tigers can cash in with Luai returning against a wounded Broncos side
Keep the charity process patient until the best number shows up

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Tigers are no longer just a novelty story
Warriors suddenly look like a serious top-end side
Storm need a response after four straight losses
Eels and Dragons are slipping into danger territory fast

betCard.title: Round 7 Charity Bet
betCard.pick: Bulldogs over Eels leads the shortlist
why: Canterbury get Leo Thompson back while Parramatta keep losing forward depth.
The Eels are coming off a 52-10 hiding and still look vulnerable through the middle.
The betting record is clean at 2-0, so the shortlist can stay disciplined while we wait for price.
risks: Stephen Crichton and Josh Curran are only in the extended squad, not the starting side.
Mitchell Moses can still swing a game if Parramatta find energy at home.
The price may shorten quickly once the public catches up to the list advantage.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.

watchlist: Tigers have turned surprise energy into a real ladder push.
Warriors just made a loud statement against Melbourne.
Storm and Eels both need to earn trust back next week.
Dragons keep sliding and are now firmly in pressure territory.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 7 opens with the board resetting, not chasing
lines: As of Tuesday, 14 April 2026, official Round 7 team lists are now out and the preview can shift from broad caution into sharper reads.
The board still comes in off a 4/8 tipping round, but the charity process stayed clean thanks to the Cowboys win and a 2-0 betting record.
The key is to let the team-list advantages guide the early leans instead of overreacting to one wild weekend.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Round 7 charity play stays uncommitted for now
lines: There is no value in pretending a Round 7 play is locked before official team lists and injury confirmations are out.
The clearest early shortlist game is Bulldogs v Eels, but price still matters before anything is locked.
The advantage right now is patience: team lists are out, but the market and final trims can still move the best spot.
With the charity ledger already at $11.50 profit, discipline still matters more than action.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who deserves the Round 7 upgrade or downgrade
lines: Hot: Tigers, Warriors, Cowboys, Bulldogs, Sea Eagles.
Melbourne and Parramatta have both moved from trust teams to prove-it teams, while Canterbury keep getting harder to oppose.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The data points most worth checking before locking Round 7
lines: Broncos, Cowboys, Tigers, Dragons, Rabbitohs and Warriors all carry injury or availability angles worth re-checking once team lists drop.
The biggest list swings are Jye Gray out for Souths, Leo Thompson in for Canterbury, Luai back for the Tigers and Reynolds named for Brisbane.
The casualty watch still includes Samuela Fainu, Tyson Frizell, Cory Paix, Murray Taulagi, Reed Mahoney and Stephen Crichton's shoulder.
Do not lock any charity play until the Tuesday 14 April 2026 team-list reaction has had time to settle through the market.

footerNotes: Built from the attached Claude prototype and reorganised into a maintainable Codex workflow.
Use this as a weekly operating board, then swap in live-verified NRL data when you want to publish or bet.
